title: Andhra Spinach Tamarind Pachadi

subs.name: sorrel (gongura) leaves if available; no tamarind needed
regular oil; less aromatic but acceptable

steps.text: Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a kadai. Add green chillies and garlic; sauté for 1 minute. Add the spinach leaves and turmeric; toss over high heat until completely wilted, about 3 minutes.
Transfer the cooked spinach to a blender and blend coarsely — the pachadi should have some texture, not be completely smooth.

Return the blended spinach to the kadai. Add tamarind pulp, chilli powder and salt. Cook on medium heat, stirring, for 8–10 minutes until the mixture thickens to a chutney-like consistency and the raw tamarind smell mellows.

In a small pan, heat the remaining oil. Add mustard seeds and let them pop. Add dried red chillies, cumin, urad dal and curry leaves; fry until the dal is golden and the leaves are crisp, about 1 minute.
Pour the tempering over the pachadi and stir through. Check salt and sourness. Serve at room temperature alongside hot rice, or use as a thick dipping chutney for dosa or pesarattu.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: spinach → sorrel (gongura) leaves if available; no tamarind needed; gingelly-oil → regular oil; less aromatic but acceptable
